error: VA may have treated a 10% Veteran as categorically ineligible.
whyItMatters: A 10% rating may support entitlement if VA finds serious employment handicap.
bestMove: Request a SEH determination and submit SEH evidence.

error: VA may have treated employment as categorically overcoming impairment.
whyItMatters: The analysis should address suitability, stability, and whether impairment has actually been overcome.
bestMove: Submit evidence that the job is unsuitable, unstable, aggravates disability, or is inconsistent with abilities, aptitudes, and interests.

error: VA may have failed to analyze service-connected disability contribution.
whyItMatters: Employment handicap requires analysis of whether service-connected disability contributes to vocational impairment.
bestMove: Submit a service-connected contribution statement and functional evidence.

error: VA may have failed to make a separate SEH determination.
whyItMatters: SEH requires separate CP/VRC determination where employment handicap is found.

error: VA may have made an unsupported infeasibility determination.
whyItMatters: Feasibility should consider supports, accommodations, and extended evaluation when appropriate.
bestMove: Request extended evaluation or submit feasibility evidence.

whyItMatters: The Veteran needs the decision, reasons, and evidence relied upon to choose the correct response.
bestMove: Request the written decision and counseling narrative.
